news.summary: U.S. stocks traded lower on Thursday, with the Dow Jones index gaining over 150 points. The Dow rose 0.40% to 42,218.43, the NASDAQ increased by 0.18% to 19,181.94, and the S&P 500 went up by 0.43% to 5,917.84. Utilities sector shares surged by 1.3%, while consumer discretionary stocks fell by 1.4%. U.S. retail sales had a slower growth rate of 0.1% in April compared to 1.7% in March. Click Holdings Limited shares rose by 168% to $0.7514 after a cooperation agreement announcement with Flash Mutual Technology. Graphjet Technology shares surged by 99% to $0.1916 following a 13D filing revealing the CEO's 48.9% stake. Foot Locker, Inc. shares were also affected.
news.headline: Crude oil prices drop by over 2%, US retail sales rise by 0.1% in April. Dow Jones up 0.40% to 42,218.43, NASDAQ up 0.18% to 19,181.94, S&P 500 up 0.43% to 5,917.84.

news.summary: Altria, a company based in Richmond, Virginia, recently held its 2025 annual meeting of shareholders. During the meeting, they announced a regular quarterly dividend of $1.02 per share.
news.headline: Altria declares $1.02 quarterly dividend at 2025 shareholders' meeting in Richmond, Virginia.

news.summary: Today, the stock market rebounded from early declines with relatively minor losses. Walmart and Alibaba faced significant selling pressure following their earnings reports. Despite this, Walmart managed to minimize its losses. The Nasdaq, S&P 500, and Russell 2000 indexes all made gains. CoreWeave also showed a strong recovery.
news.headline: Stock market recovers as Walmart and Alibaba face selling pressure post-earnings. CoreWeave rebounds.

news.summary: In San Jose, California, Calix, Inc. (NYSE: CALX) made public that NextLight, a municipal broadband service provider, has chosen to utilize Calix SmartBiz™ and the Calix Broadband Platform as their integrated small business service solution in Longmont, Colorado. NextLight, well-known for offering exceptional residential services, particularly popular among gamers, is now extending their support to local economic development using SmartBiz, a tailored managed service designed to boost productivity and efficiency.
news.headline: NextLight partners with Calix to offer SmartBiz solution for local businesses in Longmont, CO. Business aims to boost economic growth.

news.summary: On Thursday, U.S. stocks had a mixed trading session as major indices saw modest midday increases. Investors were processing new economic data indicating consumer strain and disinflationary pressure following increased tariffs in April. April retail sales only saw a 0.1% rise, slightly exceeding flat expectations. However, core retail sales, a key indicator tied closely to GDP, dropped by 0.3%, suggesting weakening consumer spending. Producer prices also decreased by 0.5% month-over-month, against predictions of an increase, marking the sharpest decline since April 2020. Defensive sectors like consumer staples and utilities fared better, reflecting a cautious market sentiment. In the tech sector, semiconductor stocks, represented by the iShares Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ: SOXX), dipped by 0.4% after a three-day uptrend triggered by the U.S.-China trade truce. Oil prices fell by 2.2%, with WTI light crude dropping to $61 per barrel. Gold prices climbed by 1.4%, exceeding $3,200.
news.headline: Stocks mixed on Wall Street as UNH falls, Foot Locker rises. Data shows consumer strain with retail sales slightly up 0.1%, core retail down 0.3%, and producer prices down 0.5% in April. Defensive sectors outperformed with some tech stocks slipping, and oil prices falling to $61/barrel. Gold climbed to above $3,200.

news.summary: A report titled "Evolution of Extended Reality (XR) in Retail, Global 2024-2030" has recently been included in the offering of ResearchAndMarkets.com. This study delves into the XR platform market's advancement within the retail sector, incorporating immersive technologies such as VR, AR, and MR. The realm of retail covers physical stores, e-commerce sites, and omnichannel sellers. XR has been significantly reshaping the retail landscape, with the global market set to expand between 2024-2030.
news.headline: Global report on XR in retail market from 2024-2030 highlights evolution and opportunities in immersive technologies. Brick-and-mortar stores, e-commerce, and omnichannel retailers benefit. Visit ResearchAndMarkets.com for more.

news.summary: Mastercard Incorporated (MA) has revealed a new partnership with MoonPay, a crypto infrastructure company, to introduce stablecoin-based payment solutions worldwide. This collaboration aims to connect traditional finance with the crypto economy by enabling the issuance of Mastercard-branded cards linked to stablecoin balances. These cards will automatically convert stablecoins into fiat currency, allowing them to be used at over 150 million merchant locations globally. This move demonstrates Mastercard's commitment to adapting to evolving financial landscapes and fostering innovation in the fintech industry.
news.headline: Mastercard partners with MoonPay to launch stablecoin-based payment solutions, targeting 100M crypto users.

news.summary: President Trump recently announced a substantial economic deal worth $1.2 trillion with Qatar, marking it as a significant development following his visit, characterized by massive agreements in aviation, defense, energy, and technology. This agreement is in addition to a $600 billion investment commitment secured in Saudi Arabia the day before. A standout from the deals is a landmark $96 billion order from Qatar Airways for Boeing and GE Aerospace, showcasing significant advancements in the aviation industry. The order includes up to 210 Boeing 787 Dreamliner and 777X aircraft, all to be powered by GE Aerospace engines. As per a White House report, this deal is anticipated to support 154,000 U.S. jobs annually, with over 1 million jobs expected to be created during production and delivery phases. President Trump emphasized the quality of products from Boeing and GE during a session with U.S. business leaders in Qatar.
news.headline: Trump secures $1.2 trillion Qatar deal with record $96 billion order from Qatar Airways for Boeing and GE, creating 154,000 U.S. jobs.

news.summary: Headquartered in San Francisco, Cognichip, a trailblazer in Artificial Chip Intelligence (ACI®) for semiconductor design, revealed its emergence from stealth mode today. The company debuted with an impressive $33 million in seed funding, spearheaded by Lux Capital and Mayfield, and backed by FPV and Candou Ventures. Cognichip introduces a groundbreaking physics-informed foundational model (PIFM) for semiconductors, aiming to significantly hasten development schedules, cut down expenses, and usher in the forthcoming era of AI technologies.
news.headline: Cognichip emerges with $33M seed funding for Artificial Chip Intelligence (ACI®), aiming to revolutionize semiconductor design.

news.summary: The slow adoption of stablecoins in Canada is causing concern among local industry observers as the country falls behind in the global market. In December 2022, the Canadian Securities Administrators classified stablecoins as securities/derivatives, leading to minimal local stablecoin issuers. Meanwhile, the US and the EU have seen significant growth in the stablecoin market due to softening regulations. This regulatory stance in Canada has led to criticism from industry experts like Morva Rohani and Tanim Rasul, who believe stablecoins should be regulated as payment instruments rather than securities. With other jurisdictions like the EU, Singapore, and the UAE introducing regulatory frameworks for stablecoins, Canada's lack of alignment may have negative implications for the Canadian dollar. Despite the benefits of stablecoins in providing cheaper P2P payments, the Canadian crypto industry faces challenges such as reputation and lack of integration into mainstream financial services. While there is a growing demand for digital payment methods in Canada, stablecoins have not gained traction due to security concerns and user preferences for existing payment methods. Liberal Prime Minister Mark Carney's past skepticism towards cryptocurrency raises doubts within the crypto industry, but his acknowledgment of stablecoins' role in payments suggests a potential for more tailored regulations to integrate stablecoins with mainstream options. The industry anticipates a regulation-focused approach towards stablecoins under Carney's leadership, aiming to modernize payments and maintain the relevance of the Canadian dollar.
news.headline: Canada falling behind in stablecoin adoption, CSA classifies them as securities. US, EU surge ahead. Concerns about CAD relevance. P2P gap in Canada. Regulations criticized for reducing growth potential, waning interest in domestic digital currency use. PM Carney cautious on cryptos. Regulatory clarity needed for stablecoin integration.

news.summary: In San Jose, California, GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. revealed a significant development. The leading designer and provider of cutting-edge 5G and 4G semiconductor solutions, the company, also known as GCT, disclosed that it has finalized a definitive agreement for the acquisition and divestiture of a total of 7,006,370 shares of its common stock. Additionally, the agreement includes warrants for acquiring up to 10,509,555 more shares of common stock. The agreed upon price for each share is $1.57, and this deal is part of a registered direct offering.
news.headline: GCT Semiconductor announces $11M direct offering, selling 7,006,370 shares at $1.57/share with warrants for 10,509,555 shares.

news.summary: Air traffic controllers successfully switched frequencies during a 90-second outage on Monday, ensuring that planes were safely separated. This incident occurred after communication issues at Newark airport. No further details on the number of planes involved or specific reasons for the outage were provided by officials.
news.headline: Colorado ATC lost contact with pilots but safely resolved situation; 90-sec outage on Monday, after Newark airport issues.

news.summary: Butler National Corporation, based in Olathe, Kansas, made an announcement on May 15, 2025, introducing Adam Sefchick, CPA, as their latest Chief Financial Officer. Butler National Corporation, which trades on the OTCQX under the ticker symbol BUKS, is well-known in the aerospace and professional services sectors. As the new CFO, Mr. Sefchick will be responsible for overseeing the financial operations of the Company. This strategic move by Butler National Corporation showcases their commitment to strengthening their leadership team.
news.headline: Butler National Corp appoints Adam Sefchick as CFO. Sefchick, CPA, to lead finance for leader in aerospace industry.
